items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital spending priorities
items.summary: Mountain View is planning its major infrastructure and facility investments for the next fiscal year. This study session determines what projects—like roads, parks, or public buildings—will get funding and when they'll be built.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission is introducing an ordinance to amend the Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect rental policies and protections for Sunnyvale renters.

items.headline: Children and Youth Services Master Plan releases annual status report
items.summary: The city presented updates on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan along with annual reports from youth empowerment programs including the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services. These reports track progress on youth services and community programs.

items.headline: Downtown historic building could get major expansion approval
items.summary: The city is considering upgrading a historic building at 405 Kipling Street from Category 4 to Category 2 status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation work. This could significantly change the structure and capacity of a downtown property.

items.headline: Figure AI opens San José office with $4.1M buildout
items.summary: Figure AI, a notable robotics and AI company, is investing $4.1M in a second-floor office at 4000 N 1st Street. This represents a new major tech player establishing a significant presence in San José.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road area
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone properties at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road and build an eight-story mixed-use building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and commercial space. This is a substantial rezone request that would transform the area and add significant housing supply.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 was cancelled, pushing the next hearing to April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street will be rescheduled for a future date. Residents interested in this development should watch for a new notice.

items.headline: Major AI company expansion at Automation Park facility
items.summary: A $7.4M tenant improvement project at 1768 Automation Parkway signals significant expansion by a major tech company in San José. This is the largest commercial project in this permit batch and indicates continued AI/tech sector growth in the city.
